question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
note: He names a concrete blocker to next-level growth: being pulled into execution details instead of operating at bigger scope.

question.label: Execution details
question.name: Execution details — where do you get pulled in most often?
question.description: Drills into the specific situations where he drops into execution so the blocker becomes concrete.

answer: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
note: He names a specific blocker: in political conversations he retreats to direct delivery, signaling a strong clarity/influence gap and mild growth constraint.

question.label: Political conversations
question.name: Political conversations — what makes those moments hard to stay in?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic that makes him retreat from influence into execution.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
note: He names a specific gap in cross-org alignment, indicating a strong clarity deficit around how to operate in political stakeholder situations. [THREAD-DEFERRED] Concrete thread: 'cross-org alignment' hesitation not drilled because drill cap requires advancing the arc.

question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

answer: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
note: He names confidence in senior forums as the stretch point, but the avoidance of looking unprepared signals mild growth hesitation and reduced willingness to lean in.

question.label: Senior forums
question.name: Senior forums — what would help you show up there with more confidence?
question.description: Drills into the specific confidence thread he named to surface the practical gap behind his hesitation.

answer: I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
note: Deficiency-as-request: he names a missing self-advocacy behaviour — not being explicit about desired opportunities — as a strong growth and ownership gap.

question.label: Wanted opportunities
question.name: What opportunities have you wanted but not said out loud?
question.description: Drills into the self-advocacy thread by naming the specific opportunities he has not been explicit about.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
note: He names a concrete growth aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which is strong positive growth signal with mild energising signal.

question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
note: He names delegation as the change needed to make room for bigger scope, which shows aspiration and ownership but also that his future picture is still constrained by current execution load. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'delegate more of the hands-on work' is a concrete thread to pick up next session.

question.label: Trade-offs ahead
question.name: What are you choosing not to own over the next six to twelve weeks?
question.description: Forces a real prioritisation conversation around strategic ownership and trade-offs.

answer: A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
note: Names a missing growth contract with milestones as the absent structure he needs, signalling strong clarity deficit and mild growth support gap. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'growth contract with milestones' surfaced, but final turn moves to the closer.

question.label: First move
question.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?
question.description: Closes by turning the conversation into one concrete next move.

answer: I want to own one strategic bet end-to-end and set a milestone check-in with stakeholders in two weeks.
note: He named a concrete next move and timeline — owning one strategic bet end-to-end with a stakeholder check-in — which is a positive commitment signal on growth and clarity.
